                         RICHARDS, WATSON & GERSHON
                              ATTORNEYS AT LAW
                         A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
                            THIRTY EIGHTH FLOOR
                                                                    RICHARD RICHARDS
                          333 SOUTH HOPE STREET
GLENN M. WATSON          LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90071-1486
ABRAHAM I. FARAR
DOUGLAS U. ARNOLD                                           SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE
DONALD J. JOHNSON        (213) 626-8484                    EIGHTEENTH FLOOR
HOWARD M. WEITZMAN       TELECOPER (213) 626-0078          101 CALIFORNIA STREET
ROGER A. LYON                                               SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94111-5821
TIMOTHY M. MANCINELLI
[ILLEGIBLE TEXT - LEFT COLUMN CONTINUES WITH MANY NAMES]
                                                            TELEX 6784664
                                                            TELECOPIER (415) 986-8722


                                    August 13, 1990

                                                            OUR FILE NUMBER
                                                            C1830-00002


VIA FAX (916) 327-2026 AND U.S. MAIL

Ms. Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807
Attn: Ms. Dixie Howard

     Re: Statement of Economic Interests
         Type: Annual
         City: Cudahy

Dear Ms. McGee:

     I acknowledge receipt of Dixie Howard's letters of June
7 (which was mis-sent to Culver City, CA) and July 23, 1990, in which
you indicate that my statement for 1989 may not comply with the
requirements of the Political Reform Act in that it does not clearly
reflect my "investment" in our law firm, although I reported income from
the firm.

     It is, and has always been, my view that I do not have
an investment in the firm within the meaning of Government Code
§82034.  Richards, Watson & Gershon is a professional
corporation whose shareholders are practicing attorneys.

     The shares issued by the corporation are minimal in
number and nominal in value. The percentage of my shares changes
as a new shareholder is admitted, as one dies or retires, or as
the Board of Directors directs to reflect each attorney's
relative value to the firm. The admission of a new shareholder
results in a minimal adjustment of the number of shares and there
a minor financial adjustment is made between the shareholders
whose number of shares is affected, on the basis of $1 per share.
That adjustment may be as little as a total of $3 or $4 in a
year, or, if there were a more substantial change, possibly as
much as $40 or $50. The total number of my shares is under 900.


AUG 13 '90 15:     2136250078   PAGE.002

SENT BY:Xerox Telecopier 7021 : 8-13-90 : 3:12PM :     2136260076-32T2025     :# 3


RICHARDS, WATSON & GERSHON

Ms. Colleen McGee
August 13, 1990
Page 2


Government Code §82034 provides in pertinent part that
"No asset shall be deemed an investment unless its fair market
value equals or exceeds one thousand dollars ($1,000)" (emphasis
added). Shares in the firm are not saleable, not even to a
shareholder in the firm and have no "fair market value." The
only market value, if any, would be the $1 per share established
by the firm for internal transfer purposes. Even using this
basis, my investment would fall below $900.

The second paragraph of your June 7, 1990 letter
relating to tax deduction requires some explanation. Richards,
Watson & Gershon is a professional corporation in which I have
substantially less than a ten percent interest. I do not receive
any fees from clients.

The Commission has received my statements of economic
interest similarly prepared in the same format that was used in
1983. I assume that the Commission's interpretation of Section
82034 has been similar to my own. Pending hearing further from
you, I have not prepared an Amendment-Schedule A as upon review
believe you will consider it to be inconsistent with past
practice.

I would appreciate hearing from you on the following
contrary to some rule or policy of the Commission of which I am
unaware. In order to avoid delay, I am faxing this letter to
you. The original is being sent to you by mail.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation.


Very truly yours,

[signature]
Glenn R. Watson

GRW:mc

SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE
EIGHTEENTH FLOOR
101 CALIFORNIA STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94111-5821
(415) 398-8484
TELECOMER (416) 986-8722

OUR FILE NUMBER

C1830-002

November 5, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Attn: Ms. Jill R. Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: 1989 Statement of Economic Interests--Cudahy
    Your File No. A-90-541

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     Since Mr. Watson is out of the country until November
19, 1990 and requesting an extension of the time for Mr. Watson
to respond to December 19, 1990, thirty days after his return.

     Please sign the enclosed copy of this letter if this
extension is acceptable and return it to me in the enclosed self-
addressed, stamped envelope. Thank you for your courtesy and
cooperation.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Michelle Clement, secretary to
                                        Glenn R. Watson

:mc
0040602

The time to respond to the request to amend Mr. Watson's 1989
Statement of Economic Interests is extended to December 19, 1990.

                                        
_______________________                 Date: November 8, 1990

[illegible handwritten text]
If there are only questions regarding the amendment
to Mr. Watson's SEI, please contact Ms. Stecher, technical assistance Division, (916) 322-5662.

It is, and has always been, my view that I do not have an investment in the firm within the meaning of Government Code §82034. Richards, Watson & Gershon is a professional corporation whose shareholders are practicing attorneys. The shares issued by the corporation are minimal in number and nominal in value. The percentage of my shares changes as a new shareholder is admitted, as one dies or retires, or as the Board of Directors directs to reflect each attorney's relative value to the firm. The admission of a new shareholder results in a minimal adjustment of the number of shares and then a minor financial adjustment is made between the shareholders whose number of shares is affected, on the basis of $1 per share. That adjustment may be as little as a total of $3 or $4 in a year, or, if there were a more substantial change, possibly as much as $40 or $50. The total number of my shares is under 900. The letter from Glenn R. Watson to Ms. Colleen McGee discusses the interpretation of Government Code §82034, which states that "No asset shall be deemed an investment unless its fair market value equals or exceeds one thousand dollars ($1,000)." Watson explains that shares in Richards, Watson & Gershon are not saleable and have no "fair market value." The only market value, if any, would be the $1 per share established by the firm for internal transfer purposes. Even using this basis, his investment would fall below $900. Watson also addresses the second paragraph of his June 7, 1990 letter regarding tax deductions. He states that Richards, Watson & Gershon is a professional corporation in which he has substantially less than a ten percent interest and does not receive any fees from clients. The Commission has received Watson's statements of economic interest annually prepared in the same format as used in 1989. Watson assumes that the Commission's interpretation of Section 82034 has been similar to his own. Pending further hearing from the Commission, he has not prepared an Amendment-Schedule A as upon review he believes it will be inconsistent with past practice. Watson expresses appreciation for hearing from the Commission if the foregoing is contrary to some rule or policy of the Commission of which he is unaware.
